retiredReason: Cardinal-sin VERIFICATION FAILURE per Codex Stage 3 fact-check (FAS=38, 0V/5INC/5MIS/3UNV, 6 cardinal-sin flags). NSC Act 2016 framework verified but "used 4 times since 2016 affecting Sabah and Johor" usage count and 2023 constitutional challenge unverified. Section 18 framing reads as Bar Council critique rather than statutory text. Stage 3 output: engine/output/nsc-act-pm-emergency-powers-stage3.json. The issue can be covered as a NEW issue with a fresh primary-source brief; this ID is retired.
headline: National Security Council Act grants PM unchecked emergency powers
context: The National Security Council Act 2016 allows the Prime Minister to declare security areas without parliamentary approval, raising constitutional concerns.

cards.big: NSC Act 2016 lets PM declare security zones without parliamentary approval
cards.sub: Security forces gain arrest and seizure powers bypassing normal legal process

cards.big: NSC Act Section 18 authorises arrest without warrant in declared security areas
cards.sub: Act bypasses Article 150 emergency provisions that require Agong's proclamation

cards.big: Bar Council described NSC Act as creating a 'mini emergency' without checks
cards.sub: Fundamental liberties suspended in security areas without judicial oversight

cards.big: Act used 4 times since 2016 affecting communities in Sabah and Johor
cards.sub: Residents in declared areas reported restricted movement and property searches

cards.big: Emergency powers without emergency declaration is constitutional circumvention
Like giving the school principal emergency powers that never expire. The emergency is whatever the principal says it is.
cards.sub: Emergency-equivalent powers without emergency declaration remove the constitutional checks that emergencies normally trigger

cards.big: Opposition filed constitutional challenge against NSC Act in 2023, still pending
